architecturalPatterns: ChatClient pattern from Spring AI for LLM interactions
Router pattern for LLM provider selection based on agent type
existingModels: Tenant (entity) - represents a tenant with userId, constitutionManual, constitutionAuto, status
TenantRun (entity) - represents a run/task execution linked to a tenant with issueType, title, status
RunTask (entity) - represents individual tasks within a run with taskId, status
RunArtifact (entity) - stores outputs from agents with agentName, artifactType, content
ConstitutionInput (DTO) - input with gitReposUrls and constitutionManual
ArchitectInput (DTO) - input with constitution and spec
DeveloperInput (DTO) - input with task, architectOutput, constitutionOutput, spec
ConstitutionOutput (DTO) - output with stack, patterns, models, endpoints, quality, tech debts, tests, conventions, dependency audit, summary
ArchitectOutput (DTO) - output with changeManagementPlan and changeTasks
DeveloperOutput (DTO) - output with changedFiles and newFiles
codeQualityConsiderations: Using Lombok to reduce boilerplate code (Getter, Setter, NoArgsConstructor)
Custom hashCode and equals implementations in entities
OffsetDateTime for timestamp columns with database-managed defaults
Enum-based status management using enumerated columns
techDebtsIdentified: Minimal test coverage - only basic Spring context load test exists
No custom repository methods beyond standard CRUD operations
No validation annotations visible on DTO input classes
No service layer implementation found in current codebase
spring-boot-devtools included in runtime scope (not recommended for production)
testCoverage: Minimal - Only one test class (BlacksmithApplicationTests) with a single contextLoads() test that verifies Spring context initialization. No unit tests for services, repositories, or controllers.

codeConventions.logging: Not explicitly observed in analyzed files
codeConventions.naming: CamelCase for Java classes, snake_case for database columns (via @Column annotations)
codeConventions.errorHandling: Not explicitly observed in analyzed files

outdated: spring-boot-devtools (marked as runtime scope with optional=true)

summary: Blacksmith is a Java 21 Spring Boot 3.5.12 application that orchestrates LLM agents (Constitution, Architect, Developer) for analyzing and modifying code repositories. It uses Spring AI with multiple LLM providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, MiniMax) routed based on agent type. The system follows a multi-tenant architecture with PostgreSQL persistence using JPA. Key entities include Tenant, TenantRun, RunTask, and RunArtifact. The application appears to be an orchestration framework without exposed REST endpoints in the current codebase. Code quality is adequate with proper JPA entity design and Lombok usage, but test coverage is minimal. The architecture supports three agent types: ConstitutionAgent analyzes repositories, ArchitectAgent creates change plans, and DeveloperAgent implements changes.
